{"credits": [{"type": "reporter", "user": {"avatar_url": "https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/137510281?v=4", "events_url": "https://api.github.com/users/baradika/events{/privacy}", "followers_url": "https://api.github.com/users/baradika/followers", "following_url": "https://api.github.com/users/baradika/following{/other_user}", "gists_url": "https://api.github.com/users/baradika/gists{/gist_id}", "gravatar_id": "", "html_url": "https://github.com/baradika", "id": 137510281, "login": "baradika", "node_id": "U_kgDOCDI9iQ", "organizations_url": "https://api.github.com/users/baradika/orgs", "received_events_url": "https://api.github.com/users/baradika/received_events", "repos_url": "https://api.github.com/users/baradika/repos", "site_admin": false, "starred_url": "https://api.github.com/users/baradika/starred{/owner}{/repo}", "subscriptions_url": "https://api.github.com/users/baradika/subscriptions", "type": "User", "url": "https://api.github.com/users/baradika", "user_view_type": "public"}}], "cve_id": "CVE-2026-47742", "cvss": {"score": 6.5, "vector_string": "CVSS:3.1/AV:N/AC:L/PR:L/UI:N/S:U/C:N/I:H/A:N"}, "cvss_severities": {"cvss_v3": {"score": 6.5, "vector_string": "CVSS:3.1/AV:N/AC:L/PR:L/UI:N/S:U/C:N/I:H/A:N"}, "cvss_v4": {"score": 0.0, "vector_string": null}}, "cwes": [{"cwe_id": "CWE-862", "name": "Missing Authorization"}], "description": "## Impact

Sub-form Livewire components used in the product editor (`Edit`, `Inventory`, `Seo`, `Shipping`, `Files`) had no authorization on their `store()` method. Any authenticated panel user, regardless of role, could mutate any product's pricing, stock, SEO metadata, shipping dimensions, and attached media without holding `edit_products`.

The affected components accepted the product ID as a public Livewire property without `#[Locked]`, so an attacker could also target an arbitrary product by tampering with the wire payload from the client.

## Patches

Fixed in `v2.8.0`. Each sub-form `store()` now authorizes against `edit_products` and the product binding is locked.

Upgrade via:

```bash
composer require shopper/admin:^2.8
```

## Workarounds

None. Upgrade to `v2.8.0`.

## References

- Pull request: https://github.com/shopperlabs/shopper/pull/511

title: Shopper: Missing authorization on Product admin Livewire sub-form components
